text: When you write code, you will inevitably encounter bugs—errors that stop your program from working correctly. Identifying bugs is the first step; read your error messages carefully, as they often point to the exact problem. A systematic approach is essential: instead of guessing, isolate the issue by testing small parts of your code one at a time. Debugging tools can be very helpful—use breakpoints to pause execution and inspect variable values, or step through your code line by line. Logging is another powerful strategy; by printing messages at key points, you can track the flow of data and see where things go wrong. With practice, these methods make debugging much less frustrating.
